description: An unchecked integer overflow in a size or index computation, producing a too-small allocation or out-of-bounds index.
body: Failure mode: a multiplication like malloc(count * size) overflows size_t and wraps to a small value, so a later loop writes far past the tiny allocation; signed overflow is additionally UB. Detection signals: UBSan reports signed-integer-overflow; a huge count yields a small allocation then a heap overflow; allocations succeed but writes corrupt the heap. Do not compute allocation sizes without overflow checks. Safer fallback: use calloc (which checks count*size) or check for overflow before multiplying (count > SIZE_MAX / size), use unsigned types for sizes, and verify with UBSan on large inputs.
